note: Empirical failure catalog from the 14-axis adversarial probe (2026-06-13). Seed for the accuracy-convergence loop. seed = synthesis-curated; extra = additional regex-fixable/over-redaction leaks harvested across probes.

seed.input: Driver's licence no. D2300-40021-77100 for the taxpayer
seed.root_cause: DL value class [A-Z0-9][A-Z0-9 -]{4,14} caps the value at 15 chars; the canonical 17-char Ontario/Quebec format A1234-NNNNN-NNNNN is truncated (leaks the final 5-digit group) or, with trailing context, backtracks to no match. This is the single highest-severity structural bug: it leaks the most common real Canadian licence format, and the lenient harness norm() masked it as a 'hit'.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: raise the value cap to ~{4,20} so a 17-char hyphenated licence fits.

seed.root_cause: DL value class includes a space, so when 'licence' anchors without consuming the optional 'number' label token, the value greedily swallows the literal word 'number ' backwards and \b then truncates the real ID mid-string. Produces a wrong span AND a partial leak of trailing groups.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: drop space from the value class (or require contiguous hyphen/digit groups) so the label word can't be absorbed; also widen the label alternation.

seed.root_cause: French label 'Permis de conduire' (and 'Permit number') is absent from the DL lookbehind alternation, which only knows English driver's/licence/DL. French-language Quebec documents leak licence numbers entirely.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add 'permis(?:\s*de\s*conduire)?' and 'permit' to the DL label alternation.

seed.input: Passport: ab123456 (as printed on the card)
seed.root_cause: PASSPORT regex /\b[A-Z]{2}\s?\d{6}\b/g has no 'i' flag, so any lowercase or mixed-case passport letters (transcribed, OCR'd, or typed) never match. Highest-yield trivial passport fix.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add the 'i' flag (note this widens the SKU/invoice over-redaction surface — pair with a guard or accept the recall/precision trade).

seed.input: Passport AB-123456 dashed on the form
seed.root_cause: Separator between letters and digits is restricted to \s? (single optional whitespace); a hyphen — common on printed/typed forms — is not allowed, so the dashed passport leaks.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: broaden the inter-group separator to [ .\-]? (mirrors SIN/phone normalization).

seed.input: His SIN is 046.454.286 according to the slip.
seed.root_cause: Both SIN recognizers hard-code the separator class as [ -]; a dot (or slash) separator between groups is never matched even with an explicit SIN label. Dotted/slashed grouping is a routine OCR/print rendering.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add '.' and '/' to the separator class on the label-anchored SIN branch (the label gates false positives, so this is safe).

seed.input: SIN: 046  454  286 with doubled spaces from the PDF.
seed.root_cause: Separator quantifier is [ -]? (max ONE char); two consecutive spaces — a routine PDF/OCR extraction artifact — exceed the single allowed separator and the match fails. Same failure class as the BN double-space leak.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: change separator to [ -]* / \s* on the label-anchored branch so multiple/zero separators are tolerated.

seed.root_cause: Grouping is rigidly 3-3-3 (\d{3}[ -]?\d{3}[ -]?\d{3}); odd OCR splits like 4-2-3 or 2-4-3 carry a clear SIN label and all 9 digits but cannot match. The BN recognizer already proves looser digit grouping is safe when an anchor is present.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: on the label-anchored SIN branch, accept a looser 9-digit run, e.g. (?:\d[ .\/-]?){8}\d, mirroring the BN approach.

seed.root_cause: Label fused to value ('SIN046454286'): the label-anchored lookbehind needs \bSIN\b + 0-6 separator chars, but 'SIN' is glued to the digits so the \b after SIN fails; the unlabeled path also can't fire \b between 'N' and '0'. Common OCR label/value merge and a dense-line concatenation case.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: relax the boundary after the label so a label glued directly to a 9-digit run still anchors.

seed.root_cause: Two valid 9-digit SINs concatenated (18 digits) have no internal \b; the unlabeled SIN regex anchors only at the outer boundary so neither 9-digit window is isolated or Luhn-tested. A sliding/anchorless 9-digit-window Luhn scan would catch both.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add an anchorless pass that extracts every 9-digit window in a long digit run and Luhn-tests it (and likewise window 9+RC+4 for BN).

seed.input: Dashed BN as some software prints it: 12-3456-789 RP 0001.
seed.root_cause: Inter-digit separator in the BN regex is [ ]? (literal ASCII space only); dashes between digit groups break the (?:\d[ ]?){8}\d run so the whole BN never matches. Legacy/export formats use dashes.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: widen the inter-digit separator to [ -]? (and the program-letter separator to allow dashes).

seed.input: Charity/import program account: 123456789 RM 0001 (non-Luhn base, no 'account' label).
seed.root_cause: BN program letters are hard-coded R[CTPZ], so RM (import/export), RR (charity), RD (excise), RN (insurance), RG (air security) accounts are not recognized. These only incidentally got caught when the 9-digit base was Luhn-valid (SIN fallback) or 'account'-labeled; with a non-Luhn base and no label they leak entirely, and the program-account suffix (e.g. 'RM 0001') is NEVER redacted even when the digits are.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: widen R[CTPZ] to R[A-Z] — the trailing 4-digit ref + \b keeps false positives low.

seed.root_cause: Each inter-digit gap allows at most one space ([ ]? per digit); OCR/PDF double-spacing between groups exceeds that and breaks the 9-digit run. (Listed in the OCR axis but the remedy is purely a separator-quantifier widening.)
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: allow 1-2 spaces between groups (e.g. [ ]{0,2} or \s* with unicode normalization upstream).

seed.input: Program ref 5 digits by typo: 123456789 RC 00001 entered.
seed.root_cause: The reference part is fixed at exactly 4 digits ((?:\d[ ]?){3}\d); a 5-digit reference fails the trailing \b and the WHOLE identifier (including the valid 9-digit BN + RC) is rejected and leaks.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: anchor the BN on the 9-digit base + R[A-Z], and treat the trailing ref as an optional/variable-length tail so a malformed ref doesn't drop the whole match.

seed.root_cause: OCR split the program letters with a space ('R C'); the anchor R[CTPZ] requires the two letters adjacent. The BN regex already tolerates fully digit-spaced bases, so R\s*[A-Z] is a consistent, low-risk widening.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: change R[CTPZ] to R\s*[A-Z] so an OCR space between the program letters doesn't drop the anchor.

seed.root_cause: The regex mandates a separator [ .\-] between exchange and line group, so a fully unseparated 10-digit phone never matches. This is the most common real-world CRM/spreadsheet-export phone form and leaks outright.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: make the inter-group separators optional ([ .\-]?) for the 10-digit NANP case; pair with a leading non-digit/boundary guard to limit collision with long IDs.

seed.input: T: (587)555-0199 with no space after paren.
seed.root_cause: After the optional \)? the regex requires a separator [ .\-] before the exchange; '(587)555-0199' has the paren directly abutting the exchange digits with no separator, so it fails. Same for '+1(204)555-0199'.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: make the separator after the (area-code) group optional when a closing paren is present.

seed.root_cause: Separator class is only [ .\-]; slash '/'-delimited phones (European-influenced / OCR form) and comma-delimited ('416,555,0142') are not matched.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add '/' and ',' to the phone separator class.

seed.root_cause: First phone is glued to 'ext204' ('0132ext204'); the trailing \b after \d{4} fails because digit->letter inside ...0132e is not a word boundary, so the first (clean) number leaks while the second is caught. Concatenation/glued-suffix boundary failure.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: replace the trailing \b with a lookahead allowing a non-digit-or-'ext' follower, so a glued extension token doesn't void the match.

seed.input: Double space between triads from PDF extraction: V6B  4N9 here.
seed.root_cause: Separator is [ -]? (zero or ONE char); two spaces between triads (routine PDF column-extraction artifact) fail and the full code leaks. TAB and slash separators fail identically.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: widen the postal separator to [ \t.\-\/]* (and add unicode spaces via upstream normalization).

seed.input: No separator at all jammed against text:codeM5S1A1onfile
seed.root_cause: The no-separator A1A1A1 form is supported only when word-bounded; the leading/trailing \b fail when the code is glued to surrounding letters ('codeM5S1A1onfile'). Glued-token boundary failure.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: replace \b with lookarounds keyed on the letter/digit alternation context rather than \w boundaries (watch for matching inside longer tokens).

seed.root_cause: The curated EN street-type list omits low-collision real Canadian types: Grove, Mews, Loop, Manor, Wynd, Landing, Gate (and Quebec FR generics Sentier, Impasse, Voie, Ruelle, Croissant). The civic-number+name+type shape never closes. These additions are low false-positive risk because they are unambiguous street generics.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add Grove|Mews|Loop|Manor|Wynd|Landing|Gate to the EN list and Sentier|Impasse|Voie|Ruelle|Croissant to the FR list.

seed.input: Habite au 5 Allee des Cedres, Laval
seed.root_cause: The FR type list is accent-SENSITIVE — it has 'Allée|Montée|Mtée|Côte' but not the accent-stripped 'Allee/Montee/Mtee/Cote' that keyboards, OCR, and legacy systems routinely produce, so the type token never matches.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add diacritic-insensitive alternations (or NFD-normalize input) for the FR type list.

seed.root_cause: Rural-route designators ('RR 3', 'Rural Route 5') have no civic-number+street-type shape and are not recognized standalone — 'RR' appears in ADDRESS_RE only as a unit suffix. Common on rural Canadian tax mail; low collision risk.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add a dedicated '\bR\.?R\.?\s*\d{1,3}\b' / 'Rural Route \d+' recognizer.

seed.input: Bureau situé au 100 3e Rue Est, Québec
seed.root_cause: Quebec ordinal grid streets ('3e Rue', '4e Avenue') put an ordinal token between the civic number and the FR type; the FR branch expects the type immediately after the number, and 'numéro 1230'/'1230, 4e Avenue' also breaks the mandatory '\d+[ \t]+' separator (no comma allowed). Type-first/number-after Quebec formats never match.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable (trickier): recognize an ordinal name token \d+(?:e|re|er) as a street name and allow a comma-tolerant civic-number separator; confine with a required type word to avoid tax-line-number false positives.

seed.root_cause: The label alternation (health card/number/no/#, PHN, OHIP, MSP, RAMQ, AHC) misses real provincial labels: Manitoba PHIN (\bPHN\b does not match 'PHIN'), Alberta AHCIP (\bAHC\b fails before 'IP'), and generic 'Medicare'/'Medicare card'/'Medicare number'. Clean 9-10 digit provincial health numbers leak.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add PHIN, AHCIP, Medicare (and relax the \b after AHC) to the HEALTH label alternation.

seed.root_cause: All French health-insurance labels ('assurance maladie', 'numéro/régime d'assurance maladie') are absent; only the acronym RAMQ is known. For a bilingual Canadian tool this is the most important HEALTH miss — RAMQ bodies would be caught if a French label anchored them.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add the French 'assurance maladie' label family to the HEALTH alternation.

seed.root_cause: The body separator class is [\d \-] (space and dash only); dot/period separators (common in OCR/print layouts) break the run so the dotted number is not matched despite the health label.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add '.' to the HEALTH body separator class (low risk given the label anchor).

seed.input: OHIP number for the dependant child claimed is 5566778899 GH
seed.root_cause: The label->number lookbehind gap is capped at [^\n\d]{0,28}; when the prose gap between label and number exceeds 28 chars the anchor expires and the number leaks. (A very long clause is the ambiguous edge; a modest widening is the regex-fixable core.)
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: widen the lookbehind gap cap (e.g. to ~40) — low FP risk because the body shape is tightly constrained.

seed.root_cause: Hidden tail leak the harness scored as PASS: the body middle is capped at {5,11} chars, so a 10-digit fully-spaced number (19 body chars) is truncated to '9 8 7 6 5 4 3' and the trailing digits leak. Real CRA-style digit-spaced forms partially leak.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: raise the body middle quantifier to cover fully-spaced 10-12 digit health numbers.

seed.root_cause: The dashed-form regex /\d{4,5}-\d{2,3}-\d{5,7}/ caps the account group at 7 digits; real Canadian accounts run to 12 (the 5-3-12 form is the named axis case), so an 8-12 digit dashed coordinate leaks. The tool leaks on its own headline format.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: widen the account group to {5,12} — but MUST be paired with a banking-context gate (see over-redaction below) or it amplifies the invoice/PO false positives.

seed.root_cause: The account label recognizer's lookbehind requires the label within [\s:#.-]{0,4} of the number; 'Bank account for refund: 0123456' has intervening words so the account leaks while transit/institution still fire — a partial leak that is worse than a full miss because it gives false confidence the line was handled.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: widen the label->value gap (carefully) and/or add 'for refund'-style filler tolerance on the account lookbehind.

seed.root_cause: The bank-account lookbehind allows only [\s:#.-]{0,4} between label and digits; '=' and '/' separators (common on EFT/void-cheque transcriptions) break the anchor and the account leaks. Same '=' / '/' gap appears in dense-line cases.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add '=' and '/' to the label-separator class on the bank-account lookbehind.

seed.root_cause: The 'Acct' label anchors but the greedy digit run \d[\d \-]{3,14}\d\b needs a trailing \b; the next char is letter 'T' (next label glued on) so digit->letter... actually the \b succeeds at digit/letter but the value over-runs — standalone 'Acct1234567' DOES detect; the glued trailing label is what breaks the span. Concatenated label-value-label chain.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: terminate the account value on a label-word lookahead so a glued following label ('Transit') doesn't void the match.

seed.input: Invoice 2024-003-0000125 issued to the client on 2024-03-15.
seed.root_cause: The dashed-form bank regex /\d{4,5}-\d{2,3}-\d{5,7}/ has NO checksum and NO label/context gate, so it equally redacts 4-3-7 invoice numbers and 5-2-7 purchase orders. This directly breaks the keep-the-financials promise, and widening the account length to fix the 5-3-12 leak makes this WORSE.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: require a nearby banking-context keyword (transit/institution/void cheque/EFT/direct deposit) for the dashed form, OR suppress on a leading 'Invoice/PO/Order/Ref' label. This gate is the prerequisite that lets the account length safely widen to {5,12}.

seed.input: Loan amortization ledger key 9876543210123452 in the GL run.
seed.root_cause: A 16-digit GL/ledger key that is Luhn-valid by coincidence (~10% of random 16-digit IDs pass Luhn) is flagged as a card. There is no IIN/BIN prefix check; a 16-19 digit run starting with 1/2/7/8/9 is not a valid card and could be rejected without harming real-card recall. Also covers the 17-digit '1234 ...' order-reference case.
seed.fixability: regex-fixable: add a BIN/IIN prefix guard (real cards start 3/4/5/6) to the CREDIT_CARD validator, rejecting Luhn-coincidence long IDs that start outside that set.

seed.input: Invoice AB123456 refers to our accounting software SKU, not a person
seed.root_cause: The unanchored PASSPORT shape (2 letters + 6 digits, no label) flags invoice numbers, SKUs, and part codes of that exact shape. Genuinely a precision/recall tension: tightening to a label anchor would miss unlabeled passport numbers. Flagged as the most important PASSPORT over-redaction because adding the 'i' flag (recall fix above) widens this surface.
seed.fixability: ambiguous (note for the loop): if the 'i' flag is added for recall, expect this FP to grow; consider a soft 'passport' label boost or a negative 'invoice/SKU/product code' context suppressor rather than a hard fix.

seed.root_cause: real-doc: label on its own line, value on next; lookbehind must cross a newline
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.root_cause: real-doc: health label on separate line
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.root_cause: real-doc: province qualifier (ON) between label and value
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.root_cause: real-doc: 7-digit passport with CA country prefix between label and value
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.input: Credit card on file: Visa 4916 0387 7292 5442  Exp 11/26
seed.root_cause: real-doc: labeled card that fails Luhn (mistyped/sample) had no label-anchored fallback
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.root_cause: real-doc: neural NER missed hyphenated French name in a form field
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.input: Corp name: Westview Dental Associates Inc.
seed.root_cause: real-doc: neural NER missed corporate name in a form field
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.root_cause: real-doc: neural NER missed employer org name
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.root_cause: real-doc: neural NER missed surname in a contact field
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.root_cause: real-doc: SIN label has words (on file) + a line break before the value; tight whitespace-only gap missed it
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

seed.input: SIN: not provided. Total income ,456.00 for the year.
seed.root_cause: worded SIN gap must not swallow a nearby dollar amount
seed.fixability: regex-fixable

extra.input: SIN: 046/454/286 as keyed by the data-entry clerk.
extra.root_cause: Slash separator not in the [ -] separator class; same root cause as dotted form — separator alphabet is too narrow for the label-anchored case.

extra.input: SIN 04 6454 286 (oddly split on the scanned form).
extra.root_cause: Grouping is hard-coded to 3-3-3 (\d{3}[ -]?\d{3}[ -]?\d{3}); a 2-4-3 split has the right 9 digits and a SIN label but cannot match. Label-anchored branch should accept a looser 9-digit run.
Same dash-in-separator gap, plus dashes around the program letter where regex uses \s* (no dash); legacy/export formats with dashes leak entirely.

extra.input: Hyphen between digits and letters only: 123456789-RT0001.
extra.root_cause: Dash before the program letter is not in \s*; the 9 contiguous digits also fail Luhn so no SIN/BANK fallback covers them.

Dot-grouped card (4-4-4-4 with '.') leaks: the separator class is only [ -], so the per-digit quantifier (?:\d[ -]?){13,19} cannot bridge dot-separated groups; each 4-digit run is < 13 min length. Dots are a common copy/paste and OCR rendering of card numbers.
Same dot-separator gap as above (4111.1111.1111.1111). Adding '.' to the separator class [ .-]? would catch it; risk is colliding with decimal amounts, but Luhn + 13-19 length keeps that low.

No-separator 16-digit run glued inside letters ('Visa4539...statement'): a letter-to-digit transition is NOT a \b word boundary, so neither anchor matches and the whole digit run is skipped. Real risk in OCR/exported text where labels run into the number. Could relax to a lookaround on non-digit instead of \b.

A 16-digit invoice ID that happens to be Luhn-valid is flagged as a card. The recognizer has no negative context check (e.g. preceding 'invoice/PO/order/ref no.'); Luhn alone cannot distinguish a card from a coincidentally-valid 16-digit business ID.

extra.input: Customer account ref 4012888888881881 in the CRM (Luhn-coincidence).
extra.root_cause: 16-digit CRM account ref equal to a known Visa test number (4012...1881) - genuinely indistinguishable from a real card by format+Luhn. Catching cards while keeping this is impossible without label context; this is the irreducible card/long-ID collision.

17-digit space-grouped Luhn-valid tracking/order reference flagged as a card. 17/18/19-digit IDs overlap the card length window; no card IIN starts '1234', so a BIN-prefix guard would reject it without harming real-card recall.

extra.input: PO 12345-99-7654321 for office supplies, not banking.
extra.root_cause: Same unanchored dashed-form regex matches a 5-2-7 purchase-order number. No left-context check ('PO', 'Invoice', etc. should suppress) and no banking-context requirement, so non-financial dashed IDs are over-redacted.

extra.input: Phone the branch at 416-555-0182 for transit details.
extra.root_cause: NOT a genuine bank-axis false positive: this was flagged as PHONE, which is correct redaction behavior. I mislabeled it as shouldNotDetect; the word 'transit' nearby does not cause the bank recognizer to fire on the phone number. Counts toward the harness over_redaction total but is a probe-design artifact, not a tool defect.
Vanity/letter-mnemonic numbers (1-800-GOT-JUNK, 1-888-CRA-TIPS) contain alpha chars; regex only matches \d, so letter-based dialables leak.
Same vanity-number class as 1-800-GOT-JUNK — alpha exchange/line segments not covered by the digit-only pattern.
Same paren-with-no-trailing-separator failure as (587)555-0199, also with +1 prefix; '(204)' directly followed by exchange digits.

extra.input: Scanned contact field: maryobrien @ accountants.ca with stray spaces.
extra.root_cause: Spaces around the '@' break the contiguous local@domain match; regex has no whitespace tolerance around the at-sign.

extra.input: Fax cover OCR: client at bob @ gmail.com per page 2.
extra.root_cause: Spaces around '@' (local ' @ ' domain) prevent the single-token match; same whitespace-tolerance gap as the prior case.

extra.input: Degraded scan shows email: r.singh@ tax.deloitte.ca
extra.root_cause: Trailing space after '@' splits local part from domain; regex demands domain char immediately after '@'.

extra.input: Accented domain: contact@québec-fiscal.ca in the French letter.
extra.root_cause: Accented 'é' in the domain is not in the ASCII-only domain class [A-Za-z0-9.-]; legitimate French/IDN domains with accents are missed (regex uses A-Za-z, not \p{L}, despite the address recognizer using \p{L}).

extra.input: Newline-split postal where space got mangled: T2N	1N4 in a table cell.
extra.root_cause: Separator class is [ -] (single literal space or hyphen only); a TAB between triads is not matched, so the full code leaks. Common in PDF/table extraction.

extra.input: Double space between triads from PDF extraction: V6B  4N9 here.
extra.root_cause: Separator is [ -]? (zero or ONE char); two spaces between triads fail, leaking the full code. Common artifact of PDF column extraction.

extra.input: Slash separator from a form template: M5S/1A1.
extra.root_cause: A slash between triads is not in [ -]; form-template slash separators leak the full code.
'Mews' missing from EN type list.
'Loop' missing from EN type list.
'Manor' missing from EN type list.
'Wynd' missing from EN type list (Scottish-origin type common in newer subdivisions).
'Landing' missing from EN type list.
'Gate' missing from EN type list.
'Sideroad' is in the list but only as a trailing type; with a trailing rural route number ('Sideroad 10') the type is no longer last so the match fails.
'Rural Route' spelled-out form is not a recognized street type and has no civic-number anchor; entirely unmatched.
FR type list only contains accented 'Montée|Mtée'; accent-stripped 'Montee' (common from keyboards/OCR/legacy systems) is absent, so the type token never matches.

extra.input: Propriete au 12 Cote du Beaver Hall, Montreal
extra.root_cause: FR type list has 'Côte' but not accent-free 'Cote'; identical accent-stripping gap as Montee/Allee.

extra.input: Le client habite au 4e Avenue numéro 1230, Limoilou
extra.root_cause: Ordinal-named street ('4e Avenue') where the civic number follows the type ('numéro 1230'); regex hard-requires a leading \d{1,6} immediately before the type, so type-first/number-after Quebec ordinal formats never match.

Civic number separated from street by a comma ('1230, 4e Avenue'); the regex's mandatory '\d+[ \t]+' separator allows only space/tab, not a comma, so the number does not connect to the ordinal street name.
Street type 'Sentier' (a standard Quebec generic) is not in the FR type list.
Street type 'Impasse' (common Quebec cul-de-sac generic) is not in the FR type list.
Street type 'Voie' is not in the FR type list.
FR-language 'Croissant' (the French rendering of Crescent, type-first order) is absent from the FR list; only English 'Crescent|Cres' exists and only in name-then-type order.

Street type 'Ruelle' (alley, common in old Montreal/Quebec City civic addresses) is not in the FR type list; note 'Rue' alone will not match because the regex requires the full type token followed by a space.

extra.input: Domicile 88 Mtee des Pionniers (abbr no accent)
extra.root_cause: Abbreviation 'Mtee' (accent-stripped 'Mtée') absent; only accented 'Mtée' is listed. Confirmed via direct test that accented 'Mtée' DOES match, isolating this to the missing accent-free spelling.

Label alternation has AHC (matches AHCIP prefix) but the \b word-boundary after AHC fails: AHC is immediately followed by 'IP', so \bAHC\b does not match inside 'AHCIP' and the lookbehind never anchors.
Generic English health label 'Medicare'/'Medicare card' is absent from the label alternation; without an anchor the spaced 10-digit number is left unredacted.

extra.input: Régime d'assurance maladie du Québec: BOUL 8888 9999 00
extra.root_cause: Same missing French label family ('régime d'assurance maladie'); the RAMQ alphanumeric body would match if anchored, but no French label anchors it.
New Brunswick 'Medicare number' label not recognized (same 'Medicare' gap); spaced 9-digit NB health number leaks.

DL value pattern [A-Z0-9][A-Z0-9 -]{4,14} caps at 15 chars; the canonical 17-char Ontario format A1234-NNNNN-NNNNN is truncated at the second hyphen, leaking the final 5-digit group in cleartext. Hit on every labeled ON-format case (A1234-12345-12345, S1500-30040-50060, W4400-12345-67890).
Label 'Permit number' is not a recognized DL anchor (the standalone word 'number' only counts when preceded by 'licence'). Provincial permits/operator IDs introduced by 'Permit' leak.

DL value char class [A-Z0-9 -] includes space, so when 'licence' anchors (without the optional 'number' in the label), the value greedily swallows the word 'number ' backwards into itself and then \b truncates the real ID mid-string, leaking the trailing groups. Wrong span AND partial leak.
Same greedy-space defect: value class allows spaces so it absorbs the literal label word 'number' and then \b cuts the value short, leaking the final space-separated group.

extra.input: Passport No. Ab 123456 per the scanned copy
extra.root_cause: Mixed-case (one lowercase letter) fails the case-sensitive [A-Z]{2} requirement. Title-cased or OCR-mixed-case passport numbers leak.

extra.input: Product code AB 123456 on the parts licence agreement
extra.root_cause: Same unanchored shape collision: a 2-letter + 6-digit product/part code is flagged PASSPORT even when the surrounding words ('Product code', 'parts') clearly indicate non-PII.

extra.input: Three valid SINs run together 046454286130692544046454286 EOL
extra.root_cause: Same concatenation failure extended to 3 glued SINs (27 digits) — no boundary between any pair, so the middle SIN never gets isolated or Luhn-checked.

Two space-grouped SINs abut with no gap ('...286130...'), so the regex consumes a misaligned 9-group run and leaves the second SIN unmatched; only the first (which also fails because the run is too long) is attempted.

SIN digits glued to the following 'BN:' label with no boundary ('046454286BN'); the BN recognizer (higher priority) claims the overlapping BN span, and the unlabeled-SIN \b can't fire mid-alnum, so the SIN leaks. Labeled-SIN lookbehind also fails because 'SIN:' is immediately followed by 9 digits then letters with no terminating \b before 'BN'.
Two CRA BN+program accounts concatenated; the BN regex's trailing \b after the 4-digit ref can't fire between '0001' and '123456789' (digit-to-digit), so the doubled run matches neither account.
BN value glued to its 'BN' label ('BN123456789RT0002'); the leading \b of the BN regex requires a boundary before the first of 9 digits, which fails between 'N' and '1'.
10-digit phone written with NO internal separator ('4165550132'); the PHONE regex mandates a separator [ .\-] between exchange and line group, so an unseparated run never matches.

Same separatorless 10-digit phone failure; two such numbers separated only by a space, neither matches because the regex requires punctuation/space between the 3-3-4 internal groups, not just at the ends.

extra.input: SIN 046.454.286 (dots inserted by the scanner OCR).
extra.root_cause: Dot used as group separator; SIN regex separator class is only [ -], so periods break the match even though the label-anchored path tolerates a failed Luhn.

extra.input: SIN 04 6454 286 stray-space regrouping (still digits, label present).
extra.root_cause: Pure digits, SIN-labelled, but regrouped 2-4-3 instead of 3-3-3; rigid \d{3}[ -]?\d{3}[ -]?\d{3} shape rejects the regrouping. BN recognizer already tolerates arbitrary internal spacing; SIN does not.

extra.input: SIN 046  454  286 with doubled spaces between groups.
extra.root_cause: Separator is a single optional [ -]; two consecutive spaces (common OCR artifact) defeat the match. Trivially widenable to [ -]* / \s* around the label-anchored path.

extra.input: SIN046454286 merged with the label by the OCR pass.
extra.root_cause: Label and digits merged into one token (SIN046454286); the lookbehind expects a word boundary + 0-6 separator chars between label and digits, and 'SIN046...' has no boundary, so neither the labelled nor unlabelled path fires (unlabelled also needs 3-3-3 grouping or Luhn on a clean run).

extra.input: Fax 416,555,0142 where commas stand in for dashes.
extra.root_cause: Comma used as separator; PHONE separator class is [ .\-] and does not include comma, so the grouped number fails to match.
